GenoSim 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Detect Runs of Homozygosity

Source: R/analysis.R
detect_roh.Rd

Scans each individual's genotype profile chromosome by chromosome to identify contiguous runs of homozygous calls (dosage 0 or 2). A limited number of heterozygous calls within a window are tolerated to account for genotyping error.

detect_roh(
  geno_matrix,
  snp_map,
  min_snps = 25L,
  min_length_bp = 1e+06,
  het_allowance = 1L
)

Arguments

geno_matrix

Integer dosage matrix \(n_{individuals} \times n_{SNPs}\).

snp_map

A data.frame with snp_id, chrom, pos_bp.

min_snps

Integer. Minimum number of SNPs in a qualifying ROH. Default 25.

min_length_bp

Numeric. Minimum ROH length in base pairs. Default 1e6.

het_allowance

Integer. Number of heterozygous calls tolerated within a run before it is broken. Default 1.

Value

A list with:

roh_segments

A data.frame of detected ROH segments with columns ind_id, chrom, start_snp, end_snp, start_bp, end_bp, length_bp, n_snps_roh.

roh_per_individual

Aggregated total ROH length and count per individual.

See also

plot_roh_per_individual

Examples

sim <- simulate_population(n_founders = 50, n_snps = 500,
                           inbreeding_F = 0.25,
                           n_generations = 2, chromosomes = 1:5, seed = 1)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 50 | SNPs: 500 | Generations: 2
#>   F=0.250 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=50
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/2
#>   -> Generation 2/2
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 236 | SNPs: 500
roh <- detect_roh(sim$genotypes[[3]], sim$snp_map, min_snps = 10)
nrow(roh$roh_segments)
#> [1] 890
